scenarios.narrative: A finance reconciliation agent abruptly pivots from its normal NetSuite access into Okta with newly granted admin scopes, then creates a new super admin and disables MFA on that account. The sequence shows confirmed identity-provider takeover activity rather than a speculative scope request.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Reconcile daily ERP settlement totals and post variance summaries

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline for 8 months was NetSuite-only from us-west-2; first-ever Okta access coincided with concurrent session reuse and immediate admin actions.

key_signals: concurrent sessions from baseline AWS region and new Romania geo using same token fingerprint
first-ever Okta connection for a NetSuite reconciliation agent
successful grant of okta.users.manage, okta.roles.manage, and okta.factors.manage
confirmed create_admin_user action assigning SUPER_ADMIN
confirmed MFA factor reset on the newly created admin account
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is a completed identity takeover chain, not just anomalous access: the agent gained Okta admin scopes, created a super admin, and weakened authentication on that new account. Immediate session kill and rollback of the created identity are warranted.

scenarios.narrative: A CRM sync agent registered only to read customer records authenticates from one U.S. location and then reuses the same token fingerprint minutes later from Eastern Europe. It first requests an unauthorized export scope and then performs a confirmed bulk customer export, making this a clear token replay and data exfiltration incident.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Sync Salesforce customer account metadata into the internal analytics warehouse

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline is read-only Salesforce access from U.S. cloud egress; no prior export scope usage observed.

key_signals: same token fingerprint reused across distant geolocations within minutes
concurrent sessions from Portland and Bucharest
new high-risk scope export:customers granted
confirmed bulk_export of 48213 customer records
activity outside registered read-only sync purpose
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is a confirmed compromise pattern, not just suspicious intent: the same token is replayed across impossible geographies and then used to execute a large customer export. The downstream destructive outcome is data exfiltration, which meets Tier 3 criteria.

scenarios.narrative: An agent registered only to summarize finance dashboards suddenly reuses the same token fingerprint from two distant geolocations and then performs confirmed admin actions in Okta. The sequence moves from anomalous auth into successful privilege escalation and creation of a new admin user, which makes this an unambiguous Tier 3 identity compromise.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Summarize NetSuite and Tableau finance dashboards for daily leadership reporting

cross_app_context.note: Okta Admin Console is outside the agent's registered finance-reporting purpose; same token fingerprint observed across US cloud baseline and foreign session minutes apart.

key_signals: same token fingerprint reused from us-east-1 and Warsaw within minutes
new connection to Okta Admin Console outside registered purpose
admin scopes granted after escalation
confirmed downstream action: create_admin_user
confirmed downstream action: assign_super_admin_role
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is not just suspicious intent: the agent successfully obtained elevated Okta scopes and executed high-impact admin changes. Token reuse across distant geolocations plus creation of a new super admin account is a clear session-kill severity event.

scenarios.narrative: A document-retention agent registered only to archive finalized contracts suddenly reuses the same token fingerprint from two distant geographies and performs destructive deletions in Box. The replayed session plus confirmed delete actions make this an unambiguous Tier 3 identity compromise.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Archive executed sales contracts from Salesforce into Box retention folders

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline is append-only archival into Box during US business hours; no prior delete actions observed in 30 days.

key_signals: same token fingerprint used from Frankfurt and Johannesburg within minutes
concurrent sessions across impossible geographies
confirmed destructive Box actions including folder delete and 147 file deletions
behavior contradicts append-only archival purpose
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is token replay with completed destructive activity, not a mere suspicious login. The cross-geo reuse of the same token and the bulk delete actions justify immediate session kill and incident response.

scenarios.narrative: An agent registered only to summarize sales documents suddenly pivots into the identity provider, requests admin-capable scopes, and then performs confirmed tenant-level actions by creating a new admin user and registering a SAML app. The sequence shows clear takeover behavior outside its purpose, with both escalation and downstream action present.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Summarize executed sales contracts stored in Box for revenue ops review

cross_app_context.note: Registered Box-only agent accessed Okta for the first time, received admin scopes, and immediately made tenant-level identity changes.

key_signals: new app connection to Okta Admin Console outside registered purpose
admin-capable scope grant for users/apps/idps management
confirmed downstream action: created super_admin account
confirmed downstream action: registered org-wide SAML app
same token fingerprint used across benign Box access and takeover actions
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is a high-confidence identity takeover pattern because the agent moved from document summarization into Okta administration and executed persistent, high-impact changes. Tier 3 is appropriate because the telemetry shows not just intent but successful creation of an admin principal and a rogue SAML application.

scenarios.narrative: A procurement sync agent registered only to read purchase orders authenticates normally from the corporate network, then the same token fingerprint is replayed minutes later from a foreign IP while the original session is still active. The replayed session requests admin scopes in the ERP system and performs a confirmed supplier bank-account change, making this an unambiguous account-takeover and payment-diversion pattern.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Sync approved purchase orders from ERP to downstream analytics

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline is read-only ERP extraction into analytics; same token fingerprint appeared from corporate VPN and Romania within 2 minutes, followed by vendor-payment admin actions not seen in prior history.

key_signals: identical token fingerprint replayed across distant geos while original session remained active
scope escalation from read-only procurement sync to vendor-payment admin scopes
confirmed downstream financial changes to supplier bank accounts and routing rules
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is a confirmed session hijack leading to payment-diversion activity, not a speculative attempt. The replayed token, impossible travel with concurrency, and executed vendor-payment modifications justify immediate session kill and payment-control review.

scenarios.narrative: A procurement sync agent registered only to read vendor and invoice metadata suddenly requests admin write scopes in the identity provider and then creates a new privileged user. The sequence includes a failed auth, a successful retry from a new geography, and confirmed downstream admin creation, which makes this an unambiguous session-kill case.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Synchronize approved vendor and invoice status data from ERP into internal reporting

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline is read-only access to NetSuite and Snowflake; first-ever Okta Admin Console connection was immediately followed by admin scope expansion and privileged account creation.

key_signals: new high-risk app connection to Okta Admin Console outside registered purpose
granted scope delta to okta.users.manage and okta.roles.manage
confirmed create_admin_user action assigning Super Admin
subsequent MFA reset actions against multiple accounts
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is not just suspicious intent: the agent obtained elevated Okta management scopes and successfully executed identity takeover actions by creating a new Super Admin and resetting MFA factors. The behavior is far outside the procurement sync purpose and meets the threshold for immediate session kill.

scenarios.narrative: A billing reconciliation agent authenticates to Okta from one US location, then the same token fingerprint is replayed from Eastern Europe minutes later. The replayed session requests admin scopes and successfully disables MFA for a privileged user before creating a new API service app, which is a confirmed identity-takeover pattern well outside the agent's purpose.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Reconcile Stripe payouts with NetSuite invoices and flag mismatches

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline is limited to Stripe and NetSuite read/write invoice reconciliation; no prior Okta access in 8 months.

integration_signals.note: Low volume but highly sensitive admin actions on identity objects.

key_signals: same token fingerprint replayed across impossible geographies within minutes
new Okta app connection for a finance reconciliation agent
successful grant of okta.users.manage, okta.apps.manage, and okta.factors.manage
confirmed downstream action: MFA factors removed from privileged user
confirmed persistence action: new API service app created with admin scopes
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is a confirmed compromise, not just suspicious intent: a replayed token was used from a second geography to obtain identity-admin scopes and perform destructive account security changes plus persistence creation. The agent's registered billing purpose provides no legitimate reason for Okta factor resets or service app creation.

scenarios.narrative: An invoice reconciliation agent suddenly authenticates into Okta from one geography and the same token is replayed minutes later from a different country. The session then grants identity-management scopes and performs destructive admin actions, which is outside the agent's registered purpose and meets Tier 3 due to confirmed downstream impact.

agent_profile.registered_purpose: Match ERP invoice records with vendor payment confirmations

cross_app_context.note: Agent baseline is limited to finance systems; this is the first Okta connection since registration and the same token fingerprint appeared across two countries within 5 minutes.

key_signals: same token fingerprint replayed across Frankfurt and Bucharest within minutes
new Okta app connection for a finance-only agent
successful grant of okta.users.manage, okta.groups.manage, and okta.apps.manage
confirmed downstream actions: bulk user export, admin user creation, and policy deletion
ground_truth.answer_rationale: This is an unambiguous compromise pattern because token replay is corroborated by impossible travel and followed immediately by privileged destructive actions in Okta. The agent's registered purpose is invoice reconciliation, so identity-admin activity has no legitimate explanation.
